slides.title: What is a GAN?
slides.content: A Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) consists of two neural networks, a Generator and a Discriminator, that work against each other in a zero-sum game framework.

slides.title: Inside a GAN
slides.content: The Generator creates data similar to real data, while the Discriminator evaluates data as real or fake.

slides.title: How Do They Compete?
slides.content: The Generator is rewarded for fooling the Discriminator, and the Discriminator is rewarded for correctly identifying fake data.

slides.title: Who Wins the Game?
slides.content: The outcome ideally is a stalemate where both networks are perfectly balanced, making it difficult for the Discriminator to distinguish real from fake data.

slides.title: Real-World Applications
slides.content:
- Image generation
- Video game development
- Text-to-image models
- Music composition

visual_assets.caption: Applications of GANs in various fields

slides.title: Challenges with GANs
slides.content:
- Training instability
- Mode collapse
- Resource intensity

visual_assets.caption: Common challenges faced while training GANs

slides.title: Future of GANs
slides.content: As GAN technology improves, we foresee enhanced capabilities in creating hyper-realistic media, bridging the gap between synthetic and real-world data.
